artifacts.claimText: University of Parma has official 2025 guidelines for responsible use of generative AI in teaching and research, approved by the Academic Senate and Board of Directors.
artifacts.normalizedValue: Official AI guidelines approved 2025-04-29; effective page date 2025-05-06

citation.citationTitle: Linee guida per l'utilizzo responsabile dell'intelligenza artificiale in ambito didattico e di ricerca
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: The guidelines treat generative AI as support for teaching, learning, and research, not a replacement for human work; AI-generated content should be examined, approved, modified, and supervised by a human author.
artifacts.normalizedValue: AI is support, not substitute; human oversight required for AI-generated content

citation.citationTitle: Linee guida per l'utilizzo responsabile dell'intelligenza artificiale in ambito didattico e di ricerca
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: For academic materials, the guidelines say use of generative AI must be explicitly declared with the tool, version, and purpose; not declaring AI use is described as a violation of academic integrity rules.
artifacts.normalizedValue: Declare AI use; non-disclosure violates academic integrity rules

citation.citationTitle: Linee guida per l'utilizzo responsabile dell'intelligenza artificiale in ambito didattico e di ricerca
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: The guidelines list non-appropriate or non-permitted academic uses of AI, including creating entire written content with AI, using unrevised AI-generated code, using AI-generated code in university exams, and using AI to evaluate exam or selection materials.
artifacts.normalizedValue: Non-appropriate uses include whole written content, unrevised AI code, AI-generated code in exams, and AI grading in exams/selections

citation.citationTitle: Linee guida per l'utilizzo responsabile dell'intelligenza artificiale in ambito didattico e di ricerca
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: The guidelines tell teachers and students to use AI tools safely and appropriately, avoiding confidential information, unpublished research data, and third-party personal data unless confidentiality, consent, or another suitable legal basis is in place.
artifacts.normalizedValue: Avoid confidential, unpublished research, and third-party personal data in AI tools unless safeguards or legal basis apply

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: l'IA deve essere finalizzata a supportare le docenti e i docenti, le studentesse e gli studenti e non sostituirsi all'attivita umana ... tutti i contenuti generati dall'IA devono sempre essere esaminati, approvati, modificati e supervisionati da un autore umano.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Linee guida per l'utilizzo responsabile dell'intelligenza artificiale in ambito didattico e di ricerca
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: L'utilizzo di IA generativa per creare materiali accademici deve essere dichiarato esplicitamente, con indicazione chiara dello strumento utilizzato, della versione e dello scopo. Non dichiarare l'uso di IA costituisce una violazione delle norme sull'integrita accademica.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Use of generative AI to create academic materials should be explicitly declared with the tool, version, and purpose; non-disclosure is a violation of academic integrity rules.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: PDF section 4.2, Come citare correttamente gli strumenti
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.citationTitle: Linee guida per l'utilizzo responsabile dell'intelligenza artificiale in ambito didattico e di ricerca
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Linee guida per l'utilizzo responsabile dell'intelligenza artificiale in ambito didattico e di ricerca
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Gli strumenti di IA non devono essere utilizzati in alcun modo che possa violare gli standard o le politiche universitarie esistenti ... non possono essere utilizzati per creare interi contenuti scritti ... non e in alcun modo consentito utilizzarli per produrre codice ... in sede di esame universitario.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The guidelines say AI tools should not violate existing university standards or policies, should not create entire written contents, and are not permitted to produce code in university exams.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: PDF section 4.1, Esempi di utilizzo considerati non appropriati e/o non consentiti

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: La responsabilita e la privacy: le docenti e i docenti, le studentesse e gli studenti devono impegnarsi a utilizzare strumenti di IA in modo sicuro e appropriato, evitando di inserire informazioni riservate ... dati di ricerche non ancora pubblicate ... non e consentito inserire dati personali di altri soggetti a meno che questi non abbiano fornito il proprio consenso.

artifacts.title: University of Parma AI Guidelines Source Review
artifacts.summary: Official University of Parma sources include a 2025 Italian PDF of guidelines for responsible use of AI in teaching and research, plus official landing/document pages. Candidate claims cover source status, human oversight, academic integrity disclosure, examples of non-appropriate uses, and privacy/data-handling cautions.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
Claims are staged agent-reviewed candidates and are not marked canonical or human reviewed.
Course-level PDFs, training announcements, and app-specific privacy pages were not used as claim evidence.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
